name: clustering
context: Redis high availability: clustering, Sentinel, replication, and failover
info: This guide covers Redis high availability and horizontal scaling. Redis Cluster partitions data across multiple master nodes using hash slots (16384 total), with each master having one or more replicas for failover. Redis Sentinel monitors master-replica topologies and performs automatic failover when a master becomes unreachable. Replication provides read scaling and data durability through asynchronous master-to-replica streaming. Client configuration differs by mode: standalone, Sentinel-managed, and Cluster. The ioredis client supports all three modes natively with automatic reconnection and slot redirection.

sections.title: Cluster mode
sections.body: Redis Cluster shards data across masters using 16384 hash slots. Each key maps to a slot via CRC16. Use `Redis.Cluster()` in ioredis with seed nodes. Multi-key operations require hash tags `{tag}` to ensure keys land on the same slot. Read scaling via `scaleReads: 'slave'`. See [[redis/caching#eviction-policies]] for per-node maxmemory configuration.

sections.title: Sentinel
sections.body: Sentinel monitors a master-replica topology and performs automatic failover via quorum voting. Deploy at least 3 Sentinel instances for reliability. Configure with `sentinels` array and `name` in ioredis. Sentinel provides simpler HA than Cluster when data fits on a single node. No hash slot restrictions on multi-key operations.

sections.title: Replication
sections.body: Asynchronous master-to-replica replication provides data durability and read scaling. Configure with `REPLICAOF` command or `replicaof` in redis.conf. Replicas are eventually consistent. Full sync on first connect, then incremental replication stream. Multiple replicas supported. Replicas can serve read-only queries while syncing.

sections.title: Failover mechanisms
sections.body: Sentinel failover: quorum detects master down, leader sentinel promotes a replica, reconfigures topology, notifies clients. Cluster failover: replicas request votes from masters via gossip, promoted replica takes over hash slots, clients receive MOVED redirections. Both modes provide automatic recovery. Configure `down-after-milliseconds` to control detection sensitivity.

sections.title: Client configuration
sections.body: ioredis supports standalone, Sentinel, and Cluster modes with the same command interface. Standalone: `new Redis(url)`. Sentinel: `new Redis({ sentinels, name })`. Cluster: `new Redis.Cluster(nodes)`. All modes handle reconnection and failover transparently. Use [[redis#connection]] URL formats for each mode.
